SD: Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrected according last EXFOR rules and Dict.
MONIT-ERR was added in Subents 2,4. Subent 5 was added
from 22506. Subents 7,8 were compiled.
Two fission chambers were contained foils of plutonium
oxide of thickness 0.12 mg/cm2 and 0.25 mg/cm2,
respectively.
The individual points have a standard
error which is generally less than 3%.
The statistical errors at higher energies are greater.
The errors arising from the uncertainties
in the normalization of the overlapping parts of the
curve amounted to 2% from 0.1 to 1.0 eV, and 3%
above 1.0 eV.
SD: MONIT-ERR was added to COMMON
Tbl.1 from J.of Nucl.Energy,v.2,p.177
5.65 mg (as Pu) of the sample were deposited on a
0.004-inch thick platinum foil over a circular area
1.60 inches in diameter and the foil was mounted in a
fission chamber.
Three different beam conditions were used in the course
of the experiment:
(i) above 0.1 eV a beam 1.38 inches in diameter was
first used, this being the largest beam-size which was
compatible with the Pu-241 foil diameter (1.60 inches);
(ii) when it appeared possible that a resonance
existed in Pu-241 at 0.255 eV, these measurements were
repeated with a smaller beam, in order to eliminate the
possibility that in certain positions of the crystal
some neutrons might have been passing outside the
boundary of the Pu-41 foil; the good agreement between
the first and second sets of measurements showed beyond
doubt that the observed resonance was real, and was not
due to any such spurious effect;
(iii) the energy region below 0.1 eV was explored with
a Pu-239 filter interposed between the crystal and the
chambers, for reasons that have already been discussed.
In the measurements on the linear accelerator,
covering the energy range 0.2 to 70 eV, the procedure
was identical with that used for the measurements
on PU-239.
The statistical accuracy of the normalization to
MONITOR points at ~0.08 eV is +-4%.
The statistical accuracy of the normalization  between
the crystal-spectrometer and linear accelerator
observations is +-6%.
